ddls:
CREATE TABLE "names" (
  "name_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each name */,
  "name" TEXT /* The baby name */,
  "frequency" INTEGER /* The frequency of the name (number of occurrences) */,
  "year" INTEGER /* The year the name was recorded */,
  "gender" TEXT /* The gender associated with the name (e.g., Male, Female) */,
  "region_id" INTEGER /* Reference to the region where the name was recorded */,
  "ethnicity" TEXT /* The ethnic background associated with the name */,
  "source_id" INTEGER /* Reference to the source of the name data */,
  "is_unique" INTEGER /* Boolean indicating if the name is unique */,
  "popularity_trend" TEXT /* A short description of the name's popularity trend */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("name_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_names_gender FOREIGN KEY ("gender") REFERENCES genders ("gender_name"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_names_region_id FOREIGN KEY ("region_id") REFERENCES regions ("region_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_names_source_id FOREIGN KEY ("source_id") REFERENCES name_sources ("source_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "regions" (
  "region_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each region */,
  "region_name" TEXT /* Name of the region */,
  "state" TEXT /* State or province where the region is located */,
  "country" TEXT /* Country where the region is located */,
  "population" INTEGER /* Population of the region */,
  "latitude" REAL /* Geographic latitude of the region */,
  "longitude" REAL /* Geographic longitude of the region */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("region_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "genders" (
  "gender_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each gender */,
  "gender_name" TEXT /* Name of the gender (e.g., Male, Female) */,
  "description" TEXT /* Description of the gender */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("gender_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "name_variations" (
  "variation_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each name variation */,
  "name_id" INTEGER /* ID of the name the variation is associated with */,
  "variation_name" TEXT /* The variation or nickname of the name */,
  "is_primary" INTEGER /* Boolean indicating if the variation is the primary name */,
  "source_id" INTEGER /* Reference to the source of the variation data */,
  "is_common" INTEGER /* Boolean indicating if the variation is commonly used */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("variation_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_name_variations_source_id FOREIGN KEY ("source_id") REFERENCES name_sources ("source_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_name_variations_name_id FOREIGN KEY ("name_id") REFERENCES names ("name_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "name_rankings" (
  "rank_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each name ranking */,
  "name_id" INTEGER /* ID of the name being ranked */,
  "year" INTEGER /* The year the ranking was recorded */,
  "region_id" INTEGER /* Reference to the region where the ranking was recorded */,
  "rank" INTEGER /* The rank of the name (1 being the most popular) */,
  "source_id" INTEGER /* Reference to the source of the ranking data */,
  "rank_change" INTEGER /* The change in rank from the previous year */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("rank_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_name_rankings_region_id FOREIGN KEY ("region_id") REFERENCES regions ("region_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_name_rankings_source_id FOREIGN KEY ("source_id") REFERENCES name_sources ("source_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_name_rankings_name_id FOREIGN KEY ("name_id") REFERENCES names ("name_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "name_histories" (
  "history_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each historical entry */,
  "name_id" INTEGER /* ID of the name being recorded */,
  "year" INTEGER /* The year the historical data is for */,
  "frequency" INTEGER /* The frequency of the name in that year */,
  "region_id" INTEGER /* Reference to the region where the data was recorded */,
  "source_id" INTEGER /* Reference to the source of the historical data */,
  "is_corrected" INTEGER /* Boolean indicating if the historical data has been corrected */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("history_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_name_histories_name_id FOREIGN KEY ("name_id") REFERENCES names ("name_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_name_histories_region_id FOREIGN KEY ("region_id") REFERENCES regions ("region_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_name_histories_source_id FOREIGN KEY ("source_id") REFERENCES name_sources ("source_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "name_trends" (
  "trend_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each trend */,
  "name_id" INTEGER /* ID of the name the trend is about */,
  "description" TEXT /* Description of the trend */,
  "start_year" INTEGER /* The year the trend started */,
  "end_year" INTEGER /* The year the trend ended */,
  "region_id" INTEGER /* Reference to the region where the trend was observed */,
  "source_id" INTEGER /* Reference to the source of the trend data */,
  "trend_strength" INTEGER /* A numeric value indicating the strength of the trend */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("trend_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_name_trends_name_id FOREIGN KEY ("name_id") REFERENCES names ("name_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_name_trends_region_id FOREIGN KEY ("region_id") REFERENCES regions ("region_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_name_trends_source_id FOREIGN KEY ("source_id") REFERENCES name_sources ("source_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "users" (
  "user_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each user */,
  "user_name" TEXT /* Full name of the user */,
  "email" TEXT /* Email address of the user */,
  "role" TEXT /* Role of the user (e.g., researcher, admin, analyst) */,
  "phone_number" TEXT /* Phone number of the user */,
  "last_login" TEXT /* Last login date and time */,
  "status" TEXT /* Status of the user (e.g., active, inactive) */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("user_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "access_logs" (
  "access_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each access event */,
  "name_id" INTEGER /* ID of the name being accessed */,
  "user_id" INTEGER /* ID of the user accessing the name */,
  "access_date" TEXT /* Date when the name was accessed */,
  "access_type" TEXT /* Type of access (e.g., view, download) */,
  "ip_address" TEXT /* IP address of the user accessing the data */,
  "user_agent" TEXT /* User agent string of the user accessing the data */,
  "is_approved" INTEGER /* Boolean indicating if the access was approved */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("access_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_access_logs_user_id FOREIGN KEY ("user_id") REFERENCES users ("user_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_access_logs_name_id FOREIGN KEY ("name_id") REFERENCES names ("name_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "name_sources" (
  "source_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each data source */,
  "source_name" TEXT /* Name of the data source */,
  "description" TEXT /* Description of the data source */,
  "contact_email" TEXT /* Contact email for inquiries about the source */,
  "data_type" TEXT /* Type of data collected (e.g., birth records, census data) */,
  "last_update" TEXT /* Last update date of the data source */,
  "is_trusted" INTEGER /* Boolean indicating if the source is trusted */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("source_id")
)
